tEn: Emergent Tool Use From Multi-Agent Autocurricula
tZh: 多智能体自动课程中的涌现工具使用
sEn: Multi-agent competition in hide-and-seek leads to emergent tool use and coordination through self-supervised autocurricula.
sZh: 通过多智能体竞争和简单的捉迷藏目标，智能体在自监督自动课程中涌现出复杂的工具使用和协作策略。
absEn: Through multi-agent competition, the simple objective of hide-and-seek, and standard reinforcement learning algorithms at scale, we find that agents create a self-supervised autocurriculum inducing multiple distinct rounds of emergent strategy, many of which require sophisticated tool use and coordination. We find clear evidence of six emergent phases in agent strategy in our environment, each of which creates a new pressure for the opposing team to adapt; for instance, agents learn to build multi-object shelters using moveable boxes which in turn leads to agents discovering that they can overcome obstacles using ramps. We further provide evidence that multi-agent competition may scale better with increasing environment complexity and leads to behavior that centers around far more human-relevant skills than other self-supervised reinforcement learning methods such as intrinsic motivation. Finally, we propose transfer and fine-tuning as a way to quantitatively evaluate targeted capabilities, and we compare hide-and-seek agents to both intrinsic motivation and random initialization baselines in a suite of domain-specific intelligence tests.
absZh: 通过多智能体竞争、简单的捉迷藏目标以及标准强化学习算法的大规模应用，我们发现智能体创造了一个自监督的自动课程，诱导出多轮不同的涌现策略，其中许多需要复杂的工具使用和协调。我们在环境中发现了智能体策略的六个涌现阶段的明确证据，每个阶段都为对手团队创造了新的适应压力；例如，智能体学会使用可移动箱子建造多物体掩体，这进而导致智能体发现可以利用斜坡克服障碍。我们进一步提供证据表明，多智能体竞争可能随着环境复杂性的增加而更好地扩展，并导致行为集中在比其他自监督强化学习方法（如内在动机）更与人类相关的技能上。最后，我们提出迁移和微调作为定量评估目标能力的方法，并在领域特定的智能测试套件中将捉迷藏智能体与内在动机和随机初始化基线进行比较。

contrib.en: Demonstrates six emergent phases of strategy in hide-and-seek via multi-agent autocurricula.
contrib.zh: 通过多智能体自动课程展示了捉迷藏中的六个涌现策略阶段。

contrib.en: Shows that multi-agent competition leads to sophisticated tool use and coordination.
contrib.zh: 表明多智能体竞争导致复杂的工具使用和协调。

contrib.en: Provides evidence that multi-agent autocurricula scale better with environment complexity than intrinsic motivation.
contrib.zh: 提供证据表明多智能体自动课程在环境复杂性上比内在动机扩展得更好。

contrib.en: Proposes transfer learning and fine-tuning as a quantitative evaluation method for targeted capabilities.
contrib.zh: 提出迁移学习和微调作为目标能力的定量评估方法。

contrib.en: Introduces a suite of domain-specific intelligence tests for evaluating agent capabilities.
contrib.zh: 引入一套领域特定的智能测试来评估智能体能力。

contrib.en: Open-sources environments and code to encourage further research in physically grounded multi-agent autocurricula.
contrib.zh: 开源环境和代码以鼓励在物理基础的多智能体自动课程方面的进一步研究。

limits.en: Strategy space is inherently bounded and may not surpass six emergent modes.
limits.zh: 策略空间固有有限，可能不会超过六种涌现模式。

limits.en: Agents require enormous experience to progress through stages due to misaligned reward functions.
limits.zh: 由于奖励函数不对齐，智能体需要大量经验才能通过阶段。

limits.en: Transfer results are mixed due to entangled skill representations that are hard to fine-tune.
limits.zh: 由于技能表示纠缠且难以微调，迁移结果参差不齐。

limits.en: Agents exploit environment design inaccuracies, such as physics simulation flaws.
limits.zh: 智能体利用环境设计的不准确性，例如物理模拟缺陷。

limits.en: Sample complexity is high; reducing it is an important direction for future research.
limits.zh: 样本复杂度高；降低它是未来研究的重要方向。

full.sec: Emergent Tool Use From Multi-Agent Autocurricula
full.secZh: 多智能体自动课程中的涌现工具使用

items.en: Through multi-agent competition, the simple objective of hide-and-seek, and standard reinforcement learning algorithms at scale, we find that agents create a self-supervised autocurriculum inducing multiple distinct rounds of emergent strategy, many of which require sophisticated tool use and coordination. We find clear evidence of six emergent phases in agent strategy in our environment, each of which creates a new pressure for the opposing team to adapt; for instance, agents learn to build multi-object shelters using moveable boxes which in turn leads to agents discovering that they can overcome obstacles using ramps. We further provide evidence that multi-agent competition may scale better with increasing environment complexity and leads to behavior that centers around far more human-relevant skills than other self-supervised reinforcement learning methods such as intrinsic motivation. Finally, we propose transfer and fine-tuning as a way to quantitatively evaluate targeted capabilities, and we compare hide-and-seek agents to both intrinsic motivation and random initialization baselines in a suite of domain-specific intelligence tests.
items.zh: 通过多智能体竞争、简单的捉迷藏目标以及大规模的标准强化学习算法，我们发现智能体创建了一个自监督的自动课程，引出了多轮不同的涌现策略，其中许多策略需要复杂的工具使用和协调。我们在环境中发现了智能体策略的六个涌现阶段的明确证据，每个阶段都为对手团队创造了新的适应压力；例如，智能体学会使用可移动的箱子建造多物体掩体，这进而导致智能体发现他们可以使用斜坡克服障碍。我们进一步提供证据表明，多智能体竞争可能随着环境复杂性的增加而更好地扩展，并产生比内在动机等其他自监督强化学习方法更以人类相关技能为中心的行为。最后，我们提出迁移和微调作为定量评估目标能力的方法，并在特定领域智能测试套件中将捉迷藏智能体与内在动机和随机初始化基线进行比较。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Emergent Skill Progression From Multi-Agent Autocurricula. Through the reward signal of hide-and-seek (shown on the y-axis), agents go through 6 distinct stages of emergence. (a) Seekers (red) learn to chase hiders, and hiders learn to crudely run away. (b) Hiders (blue) learn basic tool use, using boxes and sometimes existing walls to construct forts. (c) Seekers learn to use ramps to jump into the hiders’ shelter. (d) Hiders quickly learn to move ramps to the edge of the play area, far from where they will build their fort, and lock them in place. (e) Seekers learn that they can jump from locked ramps to unlocked boxes and then surf the box to the hiders’ shelter, which is possible because the environment allows agents to move together with the box regardless of whether they are on the ground or not. (f) Hiders learn to lock all the unused boxes before constructing their fort. We plot the mean over 3 independent training runs with each individual seed shown with a dotted line. Please see openai.com/blog/emergent-tool-use for example videos.
items.capZh: 图1：多智能体自动课程中的涌现技能进展。通过捉迷藏的奖励信号（y轴所示），智能体经历了6个不同的涌现阶段。(a) 搜索者（红色）学会追逐隐藏者，隐藏者学会粗略地逃跑。(b) 隐藏者（蓝色）学会基本的工具使用，利用箱子和有时现有的墙壁建造堡垒。(c) 搜索者学会使用斜坡跳入隐藏者的掩体。(d) 隐藏者迅速学会将斜坡移动到游戏区域的边缘，远离他们将建造堡垒的地方，并将其锁定。(e) 搜索者学会从锁定的斜坡跳到未锁定的箱子上，然后乘着箱子滑向隐藏者的掩体，这是因为环境允许智能体无论是否在地面上都能与箱子一起移动。(f) 隐藏者学会在建造堡垒之前锁定所有未使用的箱子。我们绘制了3次独立训练运行的平均值，每个种子用虚线表示。示例视频请参见openai.com/blog/emergent-tool-use。

items.en: Creating intelligent artificial agents that can solve a wide variety of complex human-relevant tasks has been a long-standing challenge in the artificial intelligence community. Of particular relevance to humans will be agents that can sense and interact with objects in a physical world. One approach to creating these agents is to explicitly specify desired tasks and train a reinforcement learning (RL) agent to solve them. On this front, there has been much recent progress in solving physically grounded tasks, e.g. dexterous in-hand manipulation (Rajeswaran et al., 2017; Andrychowicz et al., 2018) or locomotion of complex bodies (Schulman et al., 2015; Heess et al., 2017). However, specifying reward functions or collecting demonstrations in order to supervise these tasks can be time consuming and costly. Furthermore, the learned skills in these single-agent RL settings are inherently bounded by the task description; once the agent has learned to solve the task, there is little room to improve.
items.zh: 创建能够解决各种复杂人类相关任务的智能人工智能体一直是人工智能领域的长期挑战。对人类特别相关的是能够感知物理世界中的物体并与之交互的智能体。创建这些智能体的一种方法是明确指定所需任务并训练强化学习（RL）智能体来解决它们。在这方面，最近在解决物理基础任务方面取得了许多进展，例如灵巧的手内操作（Rajeswaran等人，2017；Andrychowicz等人，2018）或复杂身体的运动（Schulman等人，2015；Heess等人，2017）。然而，为了监督这些任务而指定奖励函数或收集演示可能耗时且昂贵。此外，这些单智能体RL设置中学到的技能本质上受任务描述的限制；一旦智能体学会解决任务，改进的空间就很小。

items.en: Due to the high likelihood that direct supervision will not scale to unboundedly complex tasks, many have worked on unsupervised exploration and skill acquisition methods such as intrinsic motivation. However, current undirected exploration methods scale poorly with environment complexity and are drastically different from the way organisms evolve on Earth. The vast amount of complexity and diversity on Earth evolved due to co-evolution and competition between organisms, directed by natural selection (Dawkins & Krebs, 1979). When a new successful strategy or mutation emerges, it changes the implicit task distribution neighboring agents need to solve and creates a new pressure for adaptation. These evolutionary arms races create implicit autocurricula (Leibo et al., 2019a) whereby competing agents continually create new tasks for each other. There has been much success in leveraging multi-agent autocurricula to solve multi-player games, both in classic discrete games such as Backgammon (Tesauro, 1995) and Go (Silver et al., 2017), as well as in continuous real-time domains such as Dota (OpenAI, 2018) and Starcraft (Vinyals et al., 2019). Despite the impressive emergent complexity in these environments, the learned behavior is quite abstract and disembodied from the physical world. Our work sees itself in the tradition of previous studies that showcase emergent complexity in simple physically grounded environments (Sims, 1994a; Bansal et al., 2018; Jaderberg et al., 2019; Liu et al., 2019); the success in these settings inspires confidence that inducing autocurricula in physically grounded and open-ended environments could eventually enable agents to acquire an unbounded number of human-relevant skills.
items.zh: 由于直接监督很可能无法扩展到无限复杂的任务，许多人研究了无监督探索和技能获取方法，如内在动机。然而，当前的无向探索方法在环境复杂性方面扩展性差，并且与地球上生物进化的方式截然不同。地球上巨大的复杂性和多样性是由于生物之间的共同进化和竞争，由自然选择引导而产生的（Dawkins & Krebs, 1979）。当一种新的成功策略或突变出现时，它会改变邻近智能体需要解决的隐含任务分布，并创造新的适应压力。这些进化军备竞赛创造了隐含的自动课程（Leibo等人，2019a），竞争智能体不断为彼此创造新任务。利用多智能体自动课程解决多人游戏已经取得了许多成功，无论是在经典离散游戏如西洋双陆棋（Tesauro, 1995）和围棋（Silver等人，2017）中，还是在连续实时领域如Dota（OpenAI, 2018）和星际争霸（Vinyals等人，2019）中。尽管这些环境中涌现的复杂性令人印象深刻，但学到的行为相当抽象且脱离物理世界。我们的工作继承了先前在简单物理基础环境中展示涌现复杂性的研究传统（Sims, 1994a；Bansal等人，2018；Jaderberg等人，2019；Liu等人，2019）；这些环境中的成功增强了我们的信心，即在物理基础和开放环境中诱导自动课程最终可能使智能体获得无限数量的与人类相关的技能。

items.en: We introduce a new mixed competitive and cooperative physics-based environment in which agents compete in a simple game of hide-and-seek. Through only a visibility-based reward function and competition, agents learn many emergent skills and strategies including collaborative tool use, where agents intentionally change their environment to suit their needs. For example, hiders learn to create shelter from the seekers by barricading doors or constructing multi-object forts, and as a counter strategy seekers learn to use ramps to jump into hiders’ shelter. Moreover, we observe signs of dynamic and growing complexity resulting from multi-agent competition and standard reinforcement learning algorithms; we find that agents go through as many as six distinct adaptations of strategy and counter-strategy, which are depicted in Figure 1. We further present evidence that multi-agent co-adaptation may scale better with environment complexity and qualitatively centers around more human-interpretable behavior than intrinsically motivated agents.
items.zh: 我们引入了一个新的混合竞争与合作物理基础环境，其中智能体在简单的捉迷藏游戏中竞争。仅通过基于可见性的奖励函数和竞争，智能体学习了许多涌现技能和策略，包括协作工具使用，智能体有意改变环境以适应其需求。例如，隐藏者学会通过设置路障或建造多物体堡垒来躲避搜索者，而作为反制策略，搜索者学会使用斜坡跳入隐藏者的掩体。此外，我们观察到多智能体竞争和标准强化学习算法导致的动态和不断增长的复杂性迹象；我们发现智能体经历了多达六种不同的策略和反策略适应，如图1所示。我们进一步提供证据表明，多智能体共同适应可能随着环境复杂性的增加而更好地扩展，并且在质量上比内在动机智能体更以人类可解释的行为为中心。

items.en: However, as environments increase in scale and multi-agent autocurricula become more open-ended, evaluating progress by qualitative observation will become intractable. We therefore propose a suite of targeted intelligence tests to measure capabilities in our environment that we believe our agents may eventually learn, e.g. object permanence (Baillargeon & Carey, 2012), navigation, and construction. We find that for a number of the tests, agents pretrained in hide-and-seek learn faster or achieve higher final performance than agents trained from scratch or pretrained with intrinsic motivation; however, we find that the performance differences are not drastic, indicating that much of the skill and feature representations learned in hide-and-seek are entangled and hard to fine-tune.
items.zh: 然而，随着环境规模扩大和多智能体自动课程变得更加开放，通过定性观察评估进展将变得棘手。因此，我们提出了一套针对性的智能测试，以衡量我们环境中智能体可能最终学会的能力，例如物体恒存（Baillargeon & Carey, 2012）、导航和建造。我们发现，在许多测试中，在捉迷藏中预训练的智能体比从头训练或使用内在动机预训练的智能体学习更快或达到更高的最终性能；然而，我们发现性能差异并不显著，表明在捉迷藏中学到的许多技能和特征表示是纠缠的且难以微调。

items.en: The main contributions of this work are: 1) clear evidence that multi-agent self-play can lead to emergent autocurricula with many distinct and compounding phase shifts in agent strategy, 2) evidence that when induced in a physically grounded environment, multi-agent autocurricula can lead to human-relevant skills such as tool use, 3) a proposal to use transfer as a framework for evaluating agents in open-ended environments as well as a suite of targeted intelligence tests for our domain, and 4) open-sourced environments and code222Code can be found at github.com/openai/multi-agent-emergence-environments. for environment construction to encourage further research in physically grounded multi-agent autocurricula.
items.zh: 这项工作的主要贡献是：1）明确证据表明多智能体自我对弈可以导致涌现的自动课程，其中包含许多不同且复合的策略阶段转变；2）证据表明，当在物理基础环境中诱导时，多智能体自动课程可以导致人类相关技能，如工具使用；3）提出使用迁移作为评估开放环境中智能体的框架，以及针对我们领域的一套针对性智能测试；4）开源环境和代码，以鼓励在物理基础多智能体自动课程方面的进一步研究。

items.en: There is a long history of using self-play in multi-agent settings. Early work explored self-play using genetic algorithms (Paredis, 1995; Pollack et al., 1997; Rosin & Belew, 1995; Stanley & Miikkulainen, 2004). Sims (1994a) and Sims (1994b) studied the emergent complexity in morphology and behavior of creatures that coevolved in a simulated 3D world. Open-ended evolution was further explored in the environments Polyworld (Yaeger, 1994) and Geb (Channon et al., 1998), where agents compete and mate in a 2D world, and in Tierra (Ray, 1992) and Avida (Ofria & Wilke, 2004), where computer programs compete for computational resources. More recent work attempted to formulate necessary preconditions for open-ended evolution (Taylor, 2015; Soros & Stanley, 2014). Co-adaptation between agents and environments can also give rise to emergent complexity (Florensa et al., 2017; Sukhbaatar et al., 2018; Wang et al., 2019). In the context of multi-agent RL, Tesauro (1995), Silver et al. (2016), OpenAI (2018), Jaderberg et al. (2019) and Vinyals et al. (2019) used self-play with deep RL techniques to achieve super-human performance in Backgammon, Go, Dota, Capture-the-Flag and Starcraft, respectively. Bansal et al. (2018) trained agents in a simulated 3D physics environment to compete in various games such as sumo wrestling and soccer goal shooting. In Liu et al. (2019), agents learn to manipulate a soccer ball in a 3D soccer environment and discover emergent behaviors such as ball passing and interception. In addition, communication has also been shown to emerge from multi-agent RL (Sukhbaatar et al., 2016; Foerster et al., 2016; Lowe et al., 2017; Mordatch & Abbeel, 2018).
items.zh: 在多人游戏中使用自我对弈有着悠久的历史。早期工作使用遗传算法探索自我对弈（Paredis, 1995；Pollack等人，1997；Rosin & Belew, 1995；Stanley & Miikkulainen, 2004）。Sims（1994a）和Sims（1994b）研究了在模拟3D世界中共同进化的生物形态和行为的涌现复杂性。开放进化在环境Polyworld（Yaeger, 1994）和Geb（Channon等人，1998）中进一步探索，其中智能体在2D世界中竞争和交配，以及在Tierra（Ray, 1992）和Avida（Ofria & Wilke, 2004）中，计算机程序竞争计算资源。最近的工作试图制定开放进化的必要前提条件（Taylor, 2015；Soros & Stanley, 2014）。智能体与环境之间的共同适应也可以产生涌现复杂性（Florensa等人，2017；Sukhbaatar等人，2018；Wang等人，2019）。在多智能体RL的背景下，Tesauro（1995）、Silver等人（2016）、OpenAI（2018）、Jaderberg等人（2019）和Vinyals等人（2019）使用深度RL技术的自我对弈在西洋双陆棋、围棋、Dota、夺旗和星际争霸中实现了超人类表现。Bansal等人（2018）在模拟3D物理环境中训练智能体在各种游戏中竞争，如相扑和足球射门。在Liu等人（2019）中，智能体在3D足球环境中学习操控足球，并发现涌现行为，如传球和拦截。此外，通信也被证明可以从多智能体RL中涌现（Sukhbaatar等人，2016；Foerster等人，2016；Lowe等人，2017；Mordatch & Abbeel, 2018）。

items.en: Intrinsic motivation methods have been widely studied in the literature (Chentanez et al., 2005; Singh et al., 2010). One example is count-based exploration, where agents are incentivized to reach infrequently visited states by maintaining state visitation counts (Strehl & Littman, 2008; Bellemare et al., 2016; Tang et al., 2017) or density estimators (Ostrovski et al., 2017; Burda et al., 2019b). Another paradigm are transition-based methods, in which agents are rewarded for high prediction error in a learned forward or inverse dynamics model (Schmidhuber, 1991; Stadie et al., 2015; Mohamed & Rezende, 2015; Houthooft et al., 2016; Achiam & Sastry, 2017; Pathak et al., 2017; Burda et al., 2019a; Haber et al., 2018). Jaques et al. (2019) consider multi-agent scenarios and adopt causal influence as a motivation for coordination. In our work, we utilize intrinsic motivation methods as an alternative exploration baseline to multi-agent autocurricula. Similar comparisons have also been made in Haber et al. (2018) and Leibo et al. (2019b).
items.zh: 内在动机方法在文献中得到了广泛研究（Chentanez等人，2005；Singh等人，2010）。一个例子是基于计数的探索，其中智能体通过维护状态访问计数（Strehl & Littman, 2008；Bellemare等人，2016；Tang等人，2017）或密度估计器（Ostrovski等人，2017；Burda等人，2019b）被激励访问不频繁的状态。另一个范式是基于转换的方法，其中智能体因学习的前向或逆向动力学模型中的高预测误差而获得奖励（Schmidhuber, 1991；Stadie等人，2015；Mohamed & Rezende, 2015；Houthooft等人，2016；Achiam & Sastry, 2017；Pathak等人，2017；Burda等人，2019a；Haber等人，2018）。Jaques等人（2019）考虑多智能体场景，并采用因果影响作为协调的动机。在我们的工作中，我们利用内在动机方法作为多智能体自动课程的替代探索基线。类似的比较也在Haber等人（2018）和Leibo等人（2019b）中进行过。

items.en: Tool use is a hallmark of human and animal intelligence (Hunt, 1996; Shumaker et al., 2011); however, learning tool use in RL settings can be a hard exploration problem when rewards are unaligned. For example, in Forestier et al. (2017); Xie et al. (2019) a real-world robot learns to solve various tasks requiring tools. In Bapst et al. (2019), an agent solves construction tasks in a 2-D environment using both model-based and model-free methods. Allen et al. (2019) uses a combination of human-designed priors and model-based policy optimization to solve a collection of physics-based puzzles requiring tool use. However, in each of these works, agents were explicitly incentivized to interact with and use tools, whereas in our environment agents implicitly create this incentive through multi-agent competition.
items.zh: 工具使用是人类和动物智能的标志（Hunt, 1996；Shumaker等人，2011）；然而，在RL环境中学习工具使用可能是一个困难的探索问题，当奖励不对齐时。例如，在Forestier等人（2017）；Xie等人（2019）中，真实世界机器人学习解决需要工具的各种任务。在Bapst等人（2019）中，智能体使用基于模型和无模型方法解决2D环境中的建造任务。Allen等人（2019）使用人类设计的先验和基于模型的策略优化相结合来解决一系列需要工具使用的基于物理的谜题。然而，在这些工作中，智能体被明确激励与工具交互和使用工具，而在我们的环境中，智能体通过多智能体竞争隐含地创造了这种激励。

items.en: Agents are tasked with competing in a two-team hide-and-seek game in a physics-based environment. The hiders are tasked with avoiding line of sight from the seekers, and the seekers are tasked with keeping vision of the hiders. There are objects scattered throughout the environment that the agents can grab and also lock in place. There are also randomly generated immovable rooms and walls that the agents must learn to navigate. Before the game of hide-and-seek begins, the hiders are given a preparation phase where the seekers are immobilized, giving the hiders a chance to run away or change their environment.
items.zh: 智能体的任务是在基于物理的环境中参与两队捉迷藏游戏。隐藏者的任务是避免被搜索者视线看到，搜索者的任务是保持对隐藏者的视线。环境中散布着物体，智能体可以抓取并锁定。还有随机生成的不可移动的房间和墙壁，智能体必须学会导航。在捉迷藏游戏开始之前，隐藏者有一个准备阶段，搜索者无法移动，这给了隐藏者逃跑或改变环境的机会。

items.en: There are no explicit incentives for agents to interact with objects in the environment; the only supervision given is through the hide-and-seek objective. Agents are given a team based reward; hiders are given a reward of 1 if all hiders are hidden and -1 if any hider is seen by a seeker. Seekers are given the opposite reward, -1 if all hiders are hidden and +1 otherwise. To confine agent behavior to a reasonable space, agents are penalized with a reward of -10 if they go too far outside of the play area (outside an 18 meter square). An episode lasts 240 timesteps, the first 40% of which are the preparation phase where all agents are given zero reward.
items.zh: 没有明确的激励让智能体与环境中的物体交互；唯一的监督来自捉迷藏目标。智能体获得基于团队的奖励；如果所有隐藏者都隐藏，隐藏者获得1的奖励，如果任何隐藏者被搜索者看到，则获得-1。搜索者获得相反的奖励，如果所有隐藏者都隐藏则为-1，否则为+1。为了将智能体行为限制在合理空间内，如果智能体超出游戏区域（18米正方形之外）太远，将受到-10的奖励惩罚。一个回合持续240个时间步，前40%是准备阶段，所有智能体获得零奖励。

items.en: We simulate the environment in the MUJOCO physics engine (Todorov et al., 2012). The world is populated with 1 to 3 hiders, 1 to 3 seekers, 3 to 9 movable boxes of which at least 3 are elongated, 2 movable ramps, and randomly generated static walls and rooms. We also experiment with a simpler, less randomized environment described in Appendix A.3. Agents observe the position, velocity, and size (in the case of the randomly shaped boxes) of objects and other agents. If entities are not in line-of-sight of the agent or not in a 135 degree cone in front of the agent, then they are masked out in the policy. Agents also have 30 range sensors arrayed evenly around them, similar to a lidar. In addition, each agent observes its own team and other agents’ teams as well as how much time is left in the preparation phase.
items.zh: 我们在MUJOCO物理引擎（Todorov等人，2012）中模拟环境。世界中包含1到3个隐藏者、1到3个搜索者、3到9个可移动箱子（其中至少3个是长形的）、2个可移动斜坡，以及随机生成的静态墙壁和房间。我们还实验了一个更简单、随机性较小的环境，详见附录A.3。智能体观察物体和其他智能体的位置、速度和大小（对于随机形状的箱子）。如果实体不在智能体的视线范围内或不在智能体前方135度锥形内，则在策略中被屏蔽。智能体还有30个均匀分布在周围的距离传感器，类似于激光雷达。此外，每个智能体观察自己的团队和其他智能体的团队，以及准备阶段剩余的时间。

items.en: Agents are simulated as spherical objects and have 3 action types that can be chosen simultaneously at each time step. They may move by setting a discretized force along their x𝑥x and y𝑦y axis and torque around their z𝑧z-axis. They have a single binary action to grab objects, which binds the agent to the closest object while the action is enabled. Agents may also lock objects in place with a single binary action. Objects may be unlocked only by agents on the team of the agent who originally locked the object. Agents may only grab or lock objects that are in front of them and within a small radius.
items.zh: 智能体被模拟为球形物体，在每个时间步可以同时选择3种动作类型。它们可以通过沿x轴和y轴设置离散力以及绕z轴设置扭矩来移动。它们有一个单一的二元动作来抓取物体，当动作启用时，将智能体绑定到最近的物体。智能体还可以通过单一的二元动作将物体锁定在原地。只有锁定物体的智能体所在团队的智能体才能解锁物体。智能体只能抓取或锁定它们前方且在小半径内的物体。

items.en: Agents are trained using self-play, which acts as a natural curriculum as agents always play opponents of an appropriate level.
items.zh: 智能体使用自我对弈进行训练，这充当了自然课程，因为智能体总是与适当水平的对手对战。

items.en: Agent policies are composed of two separate networks with different parameters – a policy network which produces an action distribution and a critic network which predicts the discounted future returns. Policies are optimized using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) (Schulman et al., 2017) and Generalized Advantage Estimation (GAE) (Schulman et al., 2015), and training is performed using rapid (OpenAI, 2018), a large-scale distributed RL framework. We utilize decentralized execution and centralized training. At execution time, each agent acts given only its own observations and memory state. At optimization time, we use a centralized omniscient value function for each agent, which has access to the full environment state without any information masked due to visibility, similar to Pinto et al. (2017); Lowe et al. (2017); Foerster et al. (2018).
items.zh: 智能体策略由两个具有不同参数的独立网络组成——一个产生动作分布的策略网络和一个预测折扣未来回报的评论家网络。策略使用近端策略优化（PPO）（Schulman等人，2017）和广义优势估计（GAE）（Schulman等人，2015）进行优化，训练使用大规模分布式RL框架rapid（OpenAI，2018）。我们采用分散执行和集中训练。在执行时，每个智能体仅根据其自身的观察和记忆状态行动。在优化时，我们为每个智能体使用一个集中式的全知价值函数，该函数可以访问完整的环境状态，没有任何因可见性而屏蔽的信息，类似于Pinto等人（2017）；Lowe等人（2017）；Foerster等人（2018）。

items.en: In all reported experiments, agents share the same policy parameters but act and observe independently; however, we found using separate policy parameters per agent also achieved all six stages of emergence but at reduced sample efficiency.
items.zh: 在所有报告的实验中，智能体共享相同的策略参数但独立行动和观察；然而，我们发现每个智能体使用单独的策略参数也能实现所有六个涌现阶段，但样本效率降低。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Agent Policy Architecture. All entities are embedded with fully connected layers with shared weights across entity types, e.g. all box entities are encoded with the same function. The policy is ego-centric so there is only one embedding of “self” and (#​agents−1)#agents1(\#\text{agents}-1) embeddings of other agents. Embeddings are then concatenated and processed with masked residual self-attention and pooled into a fixed sized vector (all of which admits a variable number of entities). x𝑥x and v𝑣v stand for state (position and orientation) and velocity.
items.capZh: 图2：智能体策略架构。所有实体通过全连接层嵌入，实体类型之间共享权重，例如所有箱子实体使用相同的函数编码。策略是以自我为中心的，因此只有一个“自身”的嵌入和（#智能体-1）个其他智能体的嵌入。然后嵌入被拼接，并通过带掩码的残差自注意力处理，并池化为固定大小的向量（所有这些都允许可变数量的实体）。x和v表示状态（位置和方向）和速度。

items.en: We utilize entity-centric observations (Džeroski et al., 2001; Diuk et al., 2008) and use attention mechanisms to capture object-level information (Duan et al., 2017; Zambaldi et al., 2018). As shown in Figure 2 we use a self-attention (Vaswani et al., 2017) based policy architecture over entities, which is permutation invariant and generalizes to varying number of entities. More details can be found in Appendix B.
items.zh: 我们利用以实体为中心的观察（Džeroski等人，2001；Diuk等人，2008），并使用注意力机制捕获物体级信息（Duan等人，2017；Zambaldi等人，2018）。如图2所示，我们使用基于自注意力（Vaswani等人，2017）的策略架构处理实体，该架构是排列不变的，并且可以推广到可变数量的实体。更多细节见附录B。

items.en: As agents train against each other in hide-and-seek, as many as six distinct strategies emerge, each of which creates a previously non-existing pressure for agents to progress to the next stage. Note that there are no direct incentives for agents to interact with objects or to explore, but rather the emergent strategies are solely a result of the autocurriculum induced by multi-agent competition. Figure 1 shows the progression of emergent strategies agents learn in our environment (see Appendix A.1 for trajectory traces of each strategy333See openai.com/blog/emergent-tool-use for sample videos.).
items.zh: 当智能体在捉迷藏中相互训练时，多达六种不同的策略涌现，每种策略都为智能体进步到下一阶段创造了以前不存在的压力。请注意，没有直接激励智能体与物体交互或探索，而是涌现策略完全是由多智能体竞争诱导的自动课程的结果。图1显示了智能体在我们的环境中学到的涌现策略的进展（每个策略的轨迹痕迹见附录A.1，示例视频见openai.com/blog/emergent-tool-use）。

items.en: Initially, hiders and seekers learn to crudely run away and chase. After approximately 25 million episodes of hide-and-seek, the hiders learn to use the tools at their disposal and intentionally modify their environment. They begin to construct secure shelters in which to hide by moving many boxes together or against walls and locking them in place. After another 75 million episodes, the seekers also learn rudimentary tool use; they learn to move and use ramps to jump over obstacles, allowing them to enter the hiders’ shelter. 10 million episodes later, the hiders learn to defend against this strategy; the hiders learn to bring the ramps to the edge of the play area and lock them in place, seemingly removing the only tool the seekers have at their disposal.
items.zh: 最初，隐藏者和寻找者学会粗略地逃跑和追逐。经过大约2500万次捉迷藏游戏后，隐藏者学会使用可用的工具并有意地修改环境。他们开始通过移动多个箱子或将箱子靠墙放置并锁定来建造安全的藏身之处。又经过7500万次游戏后，寻找者也学会了基本的工具使用；他们学会移动并使用坡道跳过障碍物，从而进入隐藏者的藏身之处。1000万次游戏后，隐藏者学会了防御这一策略；隐藏者学会将坡道带到游戏区域边缘并锁定，似乎移除了寻找者唯一可用的工具。

items.en: We originally believed defending against ramp use would be the last stage of emergence in this environment; however, we were surprised to find that yet two more qualitatively new strategies emerged. After 380 million total episodes of training, the seekers learn to bring a box to the edge of the play area where the hiders have locked the ramps. The seekers then use the ramp to move on top of the box and surf it to the hiders’ shelter. This is possible because the agents’ movement action allows them to apply a force on themselves regardless of whether they are on the ground or not; if they do this while grabbing the box under them, the box will move with while they are on top of it. In response, the hiders learn to lock all of the boxes in place before building their shelter.444Note that the discovery of a new skill does not necessarily correlate to the reward of a team changing. For example, the hider reward still decreases even after the discovery of surf defense, which is likely because teams with one or two hiders often do not have enough time to lock all of the boxes in play.
items.zh: 我们最初认为防御坡道使用将是该环境中涌现的最后一个阶段；然而，我们惊讶地发现又出现了两种性质上全新的策略。经过总计3.8亿次训练后，寻找者学会将一个箱子带到隐藏者锁定坡道的游戏区域边缘。然后，寻找者利用坡道移动到箱子顶部，并乘着箱子滑向隐藏者的藏身之处。这是可能的，因为智能体的移动动作允许它们对自己施加力，无论它们是否在地面上；如果它们在抓住下方箱子的同时这样做，箱子会随着它们移动。作为回应，隐藏者学会在建造藏身之处前锁定所有箱子。444注意，新技能的发现并不一定与团队奖励的变化相关。例如，即使在发现冲浪防御后，隐藏者的奖励仍然下降，这可能是因为一两个隐藏者的团队通常没有足够的时间锁定游戏中的所有箱子。

items.en: In all stages of strategy agents must learn to coordinate within their team. Similar to Liu et al. (2019), we use team-based rewards such that agents are required to collaborate in order to succeed; however, in our work we require neither population-based training (Jaderberg et al., 2017) or evolved dense rewards (Jaderberg et al., 2019). Notably, hiders learn efficient division of labor; for instance, when constructing shelter they often separately bring their own box to the construction area. Furthermore, as hiders attempt to defend against box surfing by locking boxes in place during the preparation phase, we find that 2 and 3 hider teams are able to lock 25% and 36% more boxes, respectively, than a single hider team, indicating that larger teams are able to divide labor and accomplish an objective a smaller team could not.
items.zh: 在所有策略阶段，智能体必须学会在团队内协调。与Liu等人（2019）类似，我们使用基于团队的奖励，使得智能体必须合作才能成功；然而，在我们的工作中，我们既不需要基于种群的训练（Jaderberg等人，2017），也不需要进化的密集奖励（Jaderberg等人，2019）。值得注意的是，隐藏者学会了高效的劳动分工；例如，在建造藏身之处时，它们通常分别将自己的箱子带到建造区域。此外，当隐藏者试图通过在准备阶段锁定箱子来防御冲浪时，我们发现2个和3个隐藏者的团队分别能比单个隐藏者的团队多锁定25%和36%的箱子，这表明更大的团队能够分工并完成较小团队无法完成的目标。

items.en: Many stages of emergent strategy can be mapped to behavioral shifts in the way agents interact with the tools in their environment, similar to Leibo et al. (2017); Perolat et al. (2017). We therefore track basic statistics about the agents’ interaction with objects during training, shown in Figure 3. For instance, as the hiders learn to build forts, they move and lock boxes much more during the preparation phase. Similarly, as the seekers learn to move and use ramps, the ramp movement in the main phase of the game increases, and as they learn to “box surf” there is a slight increase in the box movement during the main phase of the game. Finally, as the hiders learn to defend against this strategy by locking all boxes in place, the number of locked boxes in the preparation phase increases.
items.zh: 许多涌现策略阶段可以映射到智能体与环境工具交互方式的行为转变，类似于Leibo等人（2017）；Perolat等人（2017）。因此，我们跟踪了训练期间智能体与物体交互的基本统计数据，如图3所示。例如，当隐藏者学会建造堡垒时，它们在准备阶段移动和锁定箱子的次数大大增加。类似地，当寻找者学会移动和使用坡道时，游戏主阶段的坡道移动增加，而当它们学会“冲浪”时，游戏主阶段的箱子移动略有增加。最后，当隐藏者学会通过锁定所有箱子来防御这一策略时，准备阶段锁定的箱子数量增加。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Effect of Scale on Emergent Autocurricula. Number of episodes (blue) and wall clock time (orange) required to achieve stage 4 (ramp defense) of the emergent skill progression presented in Figure 1. Batch size denotes number of chunks, each of which consists of 10 contiguous transitions (the truncation length for backpropagation through time).
items.capZh: 图4：规模对涌现自动课程的影响。达到图1所示涌现技能进展阶段4（坡道防御）所需的游戏次数（蓝色）和挂钟时间（橙色）。批次大小表示块的数量，每个块由10个连续转换组成（通过时间反向传播的截断长度）。

items.en: We found that scale plays a critical role in enabling progression through the emergent autocurricula in hide-and-seek. The default model, which uses a batch size of 64,000 and 1.6 million parameters, requires 132.3 million episodes (31.7 billion frames) over 34 hours of training to reach stage 4 of the skill progression, i.e. ramp defense. In Figure 4 we show the effect of varying the batch size in our agents ability to reach stage 4. We find that larger batch sizes lead to much quicker training time by virtue of reducing the number of required optimization steps, while only marginally affecting sample efficiency down to a batch size of 32,000; however, we found that experiments with batch sizes of 16,000 and 8,000 never converged.
items.zh: 我们发现规模在促进捉迷藏中涌现自动课程的进展中起着关键作用。默认模型使用64,000的批次大小和160万个参数，需要1.323亿次游戏（317亿帧）和34小时的训练才能达到技能进展的阶段4，即坡道防御。在图4中，我们展示了改变批次大小对智能体达到阶段4能力的影响。我们发现，较大的批次大小通过减少所需的优化步骤数量，导致训练时间大大缩短，同时仅略微影响样本效率，直到批次大小降至32,000；然而，我们发现批次大小为16,000和8,000的实验从未收敛。

items.en: We find the emergent autocurriculum to be fairly robust as long as we randomize the environment during training. If randomization is reduced, we find that fewer stages of the skill progression emerges, and at times less sophisticated strategies emerge instead (e.g. hiders can learn to run away and use boxes as moveable shields.); see Appendix A.2 for more details. In addition, we find that design choices such as the minimum number of elongated boxes or giving each agent their own locking mechanism instead of a team based locking mechanism can drastically increase the sample complexity. We also experimented with adding additional objects and objectives to our hide-and-seek environment as well as with several game variants instead of hide-and-seek (see Appendix A.6). We find that these alternative environments also lead to emergent tool use, providing further evidence that multi-agent interaction is a promising path towards self-supervised skill acquisition.
items.zh: 我们发现，只要在训练期间随机化环境，涌现自动课程就相当稳健。如果减少随机化，我们发现技能进展的阶段较少出现，有时会出现较不复杂的策略（例如，隐藏者可以学会逃跑并使用箱子作为可移动盾牌）；更多细节见附录A.2。此外，我们发现设计选择，如最小数量的长形箱子或为每个智能体提供自己的锁定机制而不是基于团队的锁定机制，会大大增加样本复杂度。我们还尝试向捉迷藏环境添加额外物体和目标，以及使用几种游戏变体代替捉迷藏（见附录A.6）。我们发现这些替代环境也导致了涌现工具使用，进一步证明多智能体交互是迈向自监督技能获取的有前景的途径。

items.en: In the previous section we presented evidence that hide-and-seek induces a multi-agent autocurriculum such that agents continuously learn new skills and strategies. As is the case with many unsupervised reinforcement learning methods, the objective being optimized does not directly incentivize the learned behavior, making evaluation of those behaviors nontrivial. Tracking reward is an insufficient evaluation metric in multi-agent settings, as it can be ambiguous in indicating whether agents are improving evenly or have stagnated. Metrics like ELO (Elo, 1978) or Trueskill (Herbrich et al., 2007) can more reliably measure whether performance is improving relative to previous policy versions or other policies in a population; however, these metrics still do not give insight into whether improved performance stems from new adaptations or improving previously learned skills. Finally, using environment specific statistics such as object movement (see Figure 3) can also be ambiguous, e.g. the choice to track absolute movement does not illuminate which direction agents moved, and designing sufficient metrics will become difficult and costly as environments scale.
items.zh: 在前一节中，我们提供了证据表明捉迷藏引发了一个多智能体自动课程，使得智能体不断学习新技能和策略。与许多无监督强化学习方法一样，优化的目标并不直接激励所学行为，使得对这些行为的评估变得复杂。在多智能体设置中，跟踪奖励是一个不充分的评估指标，因为它可能模糊地表明智能体是否均匀进步或停滞。像ELO（Elo，1978）或Trueskill（Herbrich等人，2007）这样的指标可以更可靠地衡量性能是否相对于先前的策略版本或种群中的其他策略有所提高；然而，这些指标仍然无法揭示性能提升是源于新适应还是改进先前学习的技能。最后，使用环境特定的统计数据（如图3中的物体移动）也可能模糊不清，例如，选择跟踪绝对移动并不能说明智能体移动的方向，并且随着环境规模的扩大，设计足够的指标将变得困难和昂贵。

items.en: In Section 6.1, we first qualitatively compare the behaviors learned in hide-and-seek to those learned from intrinsic motivation, a common paradigm for unsupervised exploration and skill acquisition. In Section 6.2, we then propose a suite of domain-specific intelligence tests to quantitatively measure and compare agent capabilities.
items.zh: 在第6.1节中，我们首先定性地比较了捉迷藏中学习的行为与内在动机（一种常见的无监督探索和技能获取范式）学习的行为。在第6.2节中，我们随后提出了一套特定领域的智能测试，以定量衡量和比较智能体能力。

items.en: Intrinsic motivation has become a popular paradigm for incentivizing unsupervised exploration and skill discovery, and there has been recent success in using intrinsic motivation to make progress in sparsely rewarded settings (Bellemare et al., 2016; Burda et al., 2019b). Because intrinsically motivated agents are incentivized to explore uniformly, it is conceivable that they may not have meaningful interactions with the environment (as with the “noisy-TV” problem (Burda et al., 2019a)). As a proxy for comparing meaningful interaction in the environment, we measure agent and object movement over the course of an episode.
items.zh: 内在动机已成为激励无监督探索和技能发现的流行范式，并且最近在利用内在动机在稀疏奖励设置中取得进展方面取得了成功（Bellemare等人，2016；Burda等人，2019b）。由于内在动机驱动的智能体被激励去均匀探索，可以想象它们可能不会与环境进行有意义的交互（如“噪声电视”问题（Burda等人，2019a））。作为比较环境中有意义交互的代理，我们测量了游戏过程中智能体和物体的移动。

items.en: We first compare behaviors learned in hide-and-seek to a count-based exploration baseline (Strehl & Littman, 2008) with an object invariant state representation, which is computed in a similar way as in the policy architecture in Figure 2. Count-based objectives are the simplest form of state density based incentives, where one explicitly keeps track of state visitation counts and rewards agents for reaching infrequently visited states (details can be found in Appendix D). In contrast to the original hide-and-seek environment where the initial locations of agents and objects are randomized, we restrict the initial locations to a quarter of the game area to ensure that the intrinsically motivated agents receive additional rewards for exploring.
items.zh: 我们首先将捉迷藏中学习的行为与基于计数的探索基线（Strehl & Littman，2008）进行比较，该基线使用物体不变的状态表示，其计算方式与图2中的策略架构类似。基于计数的目标是最简单的基于状态密度的激励形式，其中显式跟踪状态访问计数，并奖励智能体访问不频繁的状态（详细信息见附录D）。与原始捉迷藏环境中智能体和物体的初始位置随机化不同，我们将初始位置限制在游戏区域的四分之一，以确保内在动机驱动的智能体因探索而获得额外奖励。

items.en: We find that count-based exploration leads to the largest agent and box movement if the state representation only contains the 2-D location of boxes: the agent consistently interacts with objects and learns to navigate. Yet, when using progressively higher-dimensional state representations, such as box location, rotation and velocity or 1-3 agents with full observation space, agent movement and, in particular, box movement decrease substantially. This is a severe limitation because it indicates that, when faced with highly complex environments, count-based exploration techniques require identifying by hand the “interesting” dimensions in state space that are relevant for the behaviors one would like the agents to discover. Conversely, multi-agent self-play does not need this degree of supervision. We also train agents with random network distillation (RND) (Burda et al., 2019b), an intrinsic motivation method designed for high dimensional observation spaces, and find it to perform slightly better than count-based exploration in the full state setting.
items.zh: 我们发现，如果状态表示仅包含箱子的2D位置，基于计数的探索会导致最大的智能体和箱子移动：智能体持续与物体交互并学会导航。然而，当使用逐渐高维的状态表示，如箱子位置、旋转和速度或1-3个智能体具有完整观察空间时，智能体移动，特别是箱子移动，显著减少。这是一个严重的限制，因为它表明，当面对高度复杂的环境时，基于计数的探索技术需要手动识别状态空间中与希望智能体发现的行为相关的“有趣”维度。相反，多智能体自博弈不需要这种程度的监督。我们还使用随机网络蒸馏（RND）（Burda等人，2019b）训练智能体，这是一种为高维观察空间设计的内在动机方法，并发现它在完整状态设置下比基于计数的探索略好。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Behavioral Statistics from Count-Based Exploration Variants and Random Network Distillation (RND) Across 3 Seeds. We compare net box movement and maximum agent movement between state representations for count-based exploration: Single agent, 2-D box location (blue); Single agent, box location, rotation and velocity (green); 1-3 agents, full observation space (red). Also shown is RND for 1-3 agents with full observation space (purple). We train all agents to convergence as measured by their behavioral statistics.
items.capZh: 图5：基于计数的探索变体和随机网络蒸馏（RND）在3个种子上的行为统计数据。我们比较了基于计数的探索在不同状态表示下的净箱子移动和最大智能体移动：单智能体，2D箱子位置（蓝色）；单智能体，箱子位置、旋转和速度（绿色）；1-3个智能体，完整观察空间（红色）。还显示了1-3个智能体具有完整观察空间的RND（紫色）。我们训练所有智能体直至收敛，以其行为统计数据衡量。

items.en: We propose to use transfer to a suite of domain-specific tasks in order to asses agent capabilities. To this end, we have created 5 benchmark intelligence tests that include both supervised and reinforcement learning tasks. The tests use the same action space, observation space, and types of objects as in the hide-and-seek environment. We examine whether pretraining agents in our multi-agent environment and then fine-tuning them on the evaluation suite leads to faster convergence or improved overall performance compared to training from scratch or pretraining with count-based intrinsic motivation. We find that on 3 out of 5 tasks, agents pretrained in the hide-and-seek environment learn faster and achieve a higher final reward than both baselines.
items.zh: 我们建议使用向特定领域任务套件的迁移来评估智能体能力。为此，我们创建了5个基准智能测试，包括监督学习和强化学习任务。这些测试使用与捉迷藏环境相同的动作空间、观察空间和物体类型。我们检查了在多智能体环境中预训练智能体，然后在评估套件上进行微调，是否比从头训练或使用基于计数的内在动机预训练导致更快的收敛或更好的整体性能。我们发现，在5个任务中的3个上，在捉迷藏环境中预训练的智能体学习更快，并达到比两个基线更高的最终奖励。

items.en: We categorize the 5 intelligence tests into 2 domains: cognition and memory tasks, and manipulation tasks. We briefly describe the tasks here; for the full task descriptions, see Appendix C. For all tasks, we reinitialize the parameters of the final dense layer and layernorm for both the policy and value networks.
items.zh: 我们将5个智能测试分为2个领域：认知与记忆任务，以及操作任务。我们在此简要描述任务；完整任务描述见附录C。对于所有任务，我们重新初始化策略网络和价值网络的最终密集层和层归一化的参数。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Fine-tuning Results. We plot the mean normalized performance and 90% confidence interval across 3 seeds smoothed with an exponential moving average, except for Blueprint Construction where we plot over 6 seeds due to higher training variance.
items.capZh: 图6：微调结果。我们绘制了3个种子上的平均归一化性能和90%置信区间，使用指数移动平均平滑，除了蓝图构建任务，由于训练方差较高，我们绘制了6个种子。

items.en: In the Object Counting supervised task, we aim to measure whether the agents have a sense of object permanence; the agent is pinned to a location and watches as 6 boxes each randomly move to the right or left where they eventually become obscured by a wall. It is then asked to predict how many boxes have gone to each side for many timesteps after all boxes have disappeared. The agent’s policy parameters are frozen and we initialize a classification head off of the LSTM hidden state. In the baseline, the policy network has frozen random parameters and only the classification head off of the LSTM hidden state is trained.
items.zh: 在物体计数监督任务中，我们旨在衡量智能体是否具有物体恒存感；智能体被固定在一个位置，观察6个箱子各自随机向左或向右移动，最终被墙壁遮挡。然后，它被要求在所有箱子消失后的多个时间步预测每个方向有多少箱子。智能体的策略参数被冻结，我们从LSTM隐藏状态初始化一个分类头。在基线中，策略网络具有冻结的随机参数，仅训练LSTM隐藏状态上的分类头。

items.en: In Lock and Return we aim to measure whether the agent can remember its original position while performing a new task. The agent must navigate an environment with 6 random rooms and 1 box, lock the box, and return to its starting position.
items.zh: 在锁定与返回任务中，我们旨在衡量智能体在执行新任务时是否能记住其原始位置。智能体必须在一个有6个随机房间和1个箱子的环境中导航，锁定箱子，然后返回其起始位置。

items.en: In Sequential Lock there are 4 boxes randomly placed in 3 random rooms without doors but with a ramp in each room. The agent needs to lock all the boxes in a particular order — a box is only lockable when it is locked in the correct order — which is unobserved by the agent. The agent must discover the order, remember the position and status of visited boxes, and use ramps to navigate between rooms in order to finish the task efficiently.
items.zh: 在顺序锁任务中，4个盒子随机放置在3个没有门但每个房间都有坡道的随机房间中。智能体需要按特定顺序锁定所有盒子——只有当盒子按正确顺序锁定时才可锁定——该顺序对智能体不可见。智能体必须发现顺序，记住已访问盒子的位置和状态，并利用坡道在房间之间导航，以高效完成任务。

items.en: Manipulation tasks: With these tasks we aim to measure whether the agents have any latent skill or representation useful for manipulating objects.
items.zh: 操作任务：通过这些任务，我们旨在衡量智能体是否具有任何可用于操作物体的潜在技能或表征。

items.en: In the Construction From Blueprint task, there are 8 cubic boxes in an open room and between 1 and 4 target sites. The agent is tasked with placing a box on each target site.
items.zh: 在蓝图构建任务中，一个开放房间内有8个立方体盒子和1到4个目标位置。智能体的任务是将一个盒子放置在每个目标位置上。

items.en: In the Shelter Construction task there are 3 elongated boxes, 5 cubic boxes, and one static cylinder. The agent is tasked with building a shelter around the cylinder.
items.zh: 在掩体构建任务中，有3个长条形盒子、5个立方体盒子和一个静态圆柱体。智能体的任务是围绕圆柱体建造一个掩体。

items.en: Results: In Figure 6 we show the performance on the suite of tasks for the hide-and-seek, count-based, and trained from scratch policies across 3 seeds. The hide-and-seek pretrained policy performs slightly better than both the count-based and the randomly initialized baselines in Lock and Return, Sequential Lock and Construction from Blueprint; however, it performs slightly worse than the count-based baseline on Object Counting, and it achieves the same final reward but learns slightly slower than the randomly initialized baseline on Shelter Construction.
items.zh: 结果：在图6中，我们展示了隐藏-寻找、基于计数和从头开始训练的策略在3个种子上的任务套件性能。隐藏-寻找预训练策略在锁与返回、顺序锁和蓝图构建任务中略优于基于计数和随机初始化的基线；然而，在物体计数任务中，它略逊于基于计数的基线；在掩体构建任务中，它获得了相同的最终奖励，但学习速度略慢于随机初始化的基线。

items.en: We believe the cause for the mixed transfer results is rooted in agents learning skill representations that are entangled and difficult to fine-tune. We conjecture that tasks where hide-and-seek pretraining outperforms the baseline are due to reuse of learned feature representations, whereas better-than-baseline transfer on the remaining tasks would require reuse of learned skills, which is much more difficult. This evaluation metric highlights the need for developing techniques to reuse skills effectively from a policy trained in one environment to another. In addition, as future environments become more diverse and agents must use skills in more contexts, we may see more generalizable skill representations and more significant signal in this evaluation approach.
items.zh: 我们认为迁移结果参差不齐的原因在于智能体学习的技能表征纠缠不清且难以微调。我们推测，隐藏-寻找预训练优于基线的任务是由于复用了学习到的特征表征，而其余任务中优于基线的迁移则需要复用学习到的技能，这要困难得多。这一评估指标凸显了开发技术以有效复用从一个环境训练的策略到另一个环境的技能的必要性。此外，随着未来环境变得更加多样化，智能体必须在更多情境中使用技能，我们可能会看到更通用的技能表征和该评估方法中更显著的信号。

items.en: In Appendix A.5 we further evaluate policies sampled during each phase of emergent strategy on the suite of targeted intelligence tasks, by which we can gain intuition as to whether the capabilities we measure improve with training, are transient and accentuated during specific phases, or generally uncorrelated to progressing through the autocurriculum. Noteably, we find the agent’s memory improves through training as indicated by performance in the navigation tasks; however, performance in the manipulation tasks is uncorrelated, and performance in object counting changes seems transient with respect to source hide-and-seek performance.
items.zh: 在附录A.5中，我们进一步评估了在涌现策略的每个阶段采样的策略在目标智能体任务套件上的表现，由此我们可以直观地了解我们衡量的能力是否随训练而提升、是否在特定阶段短暂出现并增强，或者通常与通过自动课程学习的进展无关。值得注意的是，我们发现智能体的记忆力随着训练而提升，如导航任务的表现所示；然而，操作任务的表现与之无关，而物体计数任务的表现变化似乎与源隐藏-寻找任务的表现短暂相关。

items.en: We have demonstrated that simple game rules, multi-agent competition, and standard reinforcement learning algorithms at scale can induce agents to learn complex strategies and skills. We observed emergence of as many as six distinct rounds of strategy and counter-strategy, suggesting that multi-agent self-play with simple game rules in sufficiently complex environments could lead to open-ended growth in complexity. We then proposed to use transfer as a method to evaluate learning progress in open-ended environments and introduced a suite of targeted intelligence tests with which to compare agents in our domain.
items.zh: 我们已经证明，简单的游戏规则、多智能体竞争和大规模的标准强化学习算法可以诱导智能体学习复杂的策略和技能。我们观察到多达六个不同的策略与反策略回合的涌现，这表明在足够复杂的环境中，具有简单游戏规则的多智能体自我对弈可能导致复杂性的开放式增长。然后，我们提出使用迁移作为评估开放式环境中学习进展的方法，并引入了一套目标智能体测试，用于比较我们领域中的智能体。

items.en: Our results with hide-and-seek should be viewed as a proof of concept showing that multi-agent autocurricula can lead to physically grounded and human-relevant behavior. We acknowledge that the strategy space in this environment is inherently bounded and likely will not surpass the six modes presented as is; however, because it is built in a high-fidelity physics simulator it is physically grounded and very extensible. In order to support further research in multi-agent autocurricula, we are open-sourcing our environment code.
items.zh: 我们在隐藏-寻找上的结果应被视为一个概念验证，表明多智能体自动课程可以产生物理基础且与人类相关的行为。我们承认，该环境中的策略空间固有地有限，可能不会超越所呈现的六种模式；然而，由于它构建在高保真物理模拟器中，因此具有物理基础且非常可扩展。为了支持多智能体自动课程的进一步研究，我们正在开源我们的环境代码。

items.en: Hide-and-seek agents require an enormous amount of experience to progress through the six stages of emergence, likely because the reward functions are not directly aligned with the resulting behavior. While we have found that standard reinforcement learning algorithms are sufficient, reducing sample complexity in these systems will be an important line of future research. Better policy learning algorithms or policy architectures are orthogonal to our work and could be used to improve sample efficiency and performance on transfer evaluation metrics.
items.zh: 隐藏-寻找智能体需要大量经验才能通过六个涌现阶段，这可能是因为奖励函数与最终行为并不直接对齐。虽然我们发现标准强化学习算法已经足够，但降低这些系统的样本复杂度将是未来研究的重要方向。更好的策略学习算法或策略架构与我们的工作正交，可用于提高样本效率和迁移评估指标上的性能。

items.en: We also found that agents were very skilled at exploiting small inaccuracies in the design of the environment, such as seekers surfing on boxes without touching the ground, hiders running away from the environment while shielding themselves with boxes, or agents exploiting inaccuracies of the physics simulations to their advantage. Investigating methods to generate environments without these unwanted behaviors is another import direction of future research (Amodei et al., 2016; Lehman et al., 2018).
items.zh: 我们还发现，智能体非常擅长利用环境设计中的微小不准确性，例如搜索者在不接触地面的情况下冲浪盒子、隐藏者用盒子保护自己逃离环境，或者智能体利用物理模拟的不准确性为自己谋利。研究生成没有这些不良行为的环境方法是未来研究的另一个重要方向（Amodei等人，2016；Lehman等人，2018）。

items.en: We thank Pieter Abbeel, Rewon Child, Jeff Clune, Harri Edwards, Jessica Hamrick, Joel Liebo, John Schulman and Peter Welinder for their insightful comments on this manuscript. We also thank Alex Ray for writing parts of our open sourced code.
items.zh: 我们感谢Pieter Abbeel、Rewon Child、Jeff Clune、Harri Edwards、Jessica Hamrick、Joel Liebo、John Schulman和Peter Welinder对本文的深刻评论。我们还感谢Alex Ray编写了我们开源代码的部分内容。
